subject: refactor(theme): complete DaisyUI v5 migration — all six reference phases
body:
Follow-up to the initial "infrastructure + partial" commit on this branch.
User requested no shortcuts, so this completes Phases 1-6 of the reference
doc (docs/implementations/THEME_DARK_MODE.md) in a single pass: full CSS
variable removal, component migration across 21 files, and cleanup.

Phase 1 — Audit:
  202 var(--bg-*/--text-*/--border-*/--color-*/--chart-grid/--glow-*/--shadow*)
  references in styles.css, 166 text-themed-*/bg-themed-*/border-themed
  references across 21 JSX files, 39 dark: Tailwind pairs across 10 JSX
  files, and ~15 hardcoded rgba(45, 104, 255, ...) brand-blue values.

Phase 2 — Custom variable removal (dashboard/styles.css):
  Rewrote :root to hold ONLY non-theme tokens (spacing, radius, z-index,
  fonts). Deleted the entire html.dark override block — DaisyUI's
  [data-theme] selectors handle switching now. Bulk-replaced via
  replace_all:
    var(--bg-primary/secondary/tertiary/hover) →
      var(--color-base-100/200/300/300)
    var(--text-primary) → var(--color-base-content)
    var(--text-secondary/tertiary/muted) →
      color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-base-content) 80%/60%/40%, transparent)
    var(--border-color/light) → var(--color-base-300/200)
    var(--glow-primary) →
      0 0 20px color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 25%, transparent)
    var(--chart-grid) →
      color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-base-content) 10%, transparent)
    var(--color-primary-alpha) →
      color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 10%, transparent)

  Replaced hardcoded brand-blue rgba(45, 104, 255, X) values with color-mix
  against var(--color-primary) — focus rings, btn-primary hover, drop-zone
  states, tab-btn-active text-shadow, body::before techy grid background.
  The brand accent now follows the active theme's primary color.

  Deleted 3 legacy CSS blocks: Tailwind gray overrides (.bg-gray-50/100,
  .text-gray-400/500/600/700/900, .border-gray-100/200, etc.), semantic
  card backgrounds (.bg-amber-50, .bg-indigo-50, ..., .bg-red-50), and
  semantic text colors (.text-green-600, .text-red-600, .text-amber-600,
  .text-blue-600, .border-blue-500).

  Deleted 8 utility class definitions: .text-themed-primary/secondary/
  tertiary/muted, .bg-themed-primary/secondary/tertiary, .border-themed.

  Swapped color: white / #1a1a1a / #fff hardcoded foregrounds to DaisyUI
  *-content tokens in .toast-success/error/warning, .btn-primary,
  .quick-guide-btn-primary, .filter-preset-btn:hover/.active,
  .filter-mode-toggle button.active, .project-link-primary, .filter-badge.

  .install-modal-note amber rgba literals → color-mix against warning.
  .drop-zone-heading color: #e5e7eb → var(--color-base-content).

  Print styles @media print intentionally keep hardcoded white/black/#e5e7eb
  per the reference doc's Print Override section.

Phase 3 — Component migration (21 JSX files, all 39 dark: pairs):
  Zero dark: class names remain in JSX (only in migration comments).

  166 text-themed-*/bg-themed-*/border-themed references migrated to
  DaisyUI Tailwind utilities:
    text-themed-primary → text-base-content
    text-themed-secondary/tertiary/muted →
      text-base-content/80, /60, /40
    bg-themed-primary/secondary/tertiary → bg-base-100/200/300
    border-themed → border-base-300

  Summary.jsx Activity Snapshot cards: 4 data viz pastel bg/dark pairs →
    bg-warning/15 + text-warning (after-hours),
    bg-info/15 + text-info (weekend),
    bg-accent/15 + text-accent (holiday),
    bg-secondary/15 + text-secondary (complex).
  Timeline.jsx complexity badges: purple/blue/gray tier colors →
    bg-secondary/20 (high) / bg-info/20 (medium) / bg-base-300 (low).
  Health.jsx security panels: all bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900/20 + border-
    red-200/800 + text-red-600/400 → bg-error/10 + border-error/40
    + text-error.
  HealthBars, HealthAnomalies, Contributors, Tags, Timing, Progress,
    Discover: progress-bar rails → bg-base-300, hover states →
    hover:bg-base-200 / hover:bg-base-300.

  App.jsx embed override now sets --color-base-100 (DaisyUI token) instead
  of the removed --bg-primary alias, and toggles data-theme alongside .dark.

  Tags.jsx removed its per-component useChartTextColor hook and explicit
  color: CHART_TEXT_COLOR overrides — now relies on ChartJS.defaults.color
  (kept fresh by AppContext's darkMode effect) as the single source of
  truth. Chart rebuilds on theme toggle via existing state.darkMode useMemo
  dep.

Chart.js theme sync:
  AppContext.jsx darkMode effect no longer reads removed --text-secondary /
  --chart-grid. It reads DaisyUI's --color-base-content oklch token
  directly and passes color-mix(in oklab, X 80%/10%, transparent) to
  ChartJS.defaults.color / borderColor. Canvas parses color-mix() since
  Chrome 111 / Firefox 113 / Safari 16.2 — same baseline as DaisyUI.

Phase 5 — Verification:
  TESTING_GUIDE.md Theme section rewritten with full 10-point checklist:
  system preference, dual-layer attributes, flash prevention, cross-tab
  sync, PWA status bar, charts, surface auto-switching, semantic tokens,
  print override. Build passes and Vite preview serves correctly — DaisyUI
  [data-theme="lofi"] and [data-theme="black"] selectors present in the
  served CSS (verified via curl).

Phase 6 — Cleanup:
  Zero custom --bg-*/--text-*/--border-*/--color-primary-alpha/--chart-grid/
  --glow-*/--shadow* variables remain anywhere in the codebase (verified
  via comprehensive Grep — only remaining matches are migration comments).
  Updated CLAUDE.md Frontend standards to enforce DaisyUI semantic tokens
  and prohibit re-introducing the deleted variables.

Build: passes. CSS bundle 147.16 KB → 123.27 KB (−23.9 KB, −16%) from
deleted variables, gray overrides, and utility classes.

Docs updated: CLAUDE.md, docs/SESSION_NOTES.md, docs/HISTORY.md,
docs/TODO.md, docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md, docs/USER_GUIDE.md.

Remaining (optional) future work: replace raw Tailwind / custom-class
elements with DaisyUI component classes (btn, card, input, badge) —
tracked in docs/TODO.md. Not required for the migration to be complete.

Tags: theme, daisyui, refactor, ui, css, cleanup
Complexity: 5
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-migration
Semver: minor
